content.problemStatement:
A permutation PPP of length NNN (111-based) is called good if it satisfies the following these conditions:


For each 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N, Pi≠iP_i \neq iPi​=i
∣P1−1∣⊕∣P2−2∣⊕…⊕∣PN−N∣=0|P_1 - 1| \oplus |P_2 - 2| \oplus \ldots \oplus |P_N - N| = 0∣P1​−1∣⊕∣P2​−2∣⊕…⊕∣PN​−N∣=0
Here, ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise XOR operation and ∣X∣|X|∣X∣ denotes the absolute value of XXX.

Find any good permutation PPP of length NNN or print −1-1−1 if no such permutation exists.

Note: A permutation of length NNN is an array which contains every integer from 111 to NNN (inclusive) exactly once.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the permutation PPP.


Output Format
For each test case, print −1-1−1 if no such permutation exists. Otherwise, in a single line, output NNN space-separated integers - the required good permutation. If more than one solution exists, you may print any of them.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the length of the required permutation.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output -1 on a single line if no good permutation exists. Otherwise output N space‑separated integers representing any good permutation.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Even N are solved by swapping each adjacent pair, giving XOR 0. Odd N have no solution, so -1 is printed.
Both N are even; each adjacent pair is swapped.
A single element permutation cannot avoid a fixed point.

solution.approach: A permutation where every element is swapped with its neighbour (i.e., P[i]=i+1 and P[i+1]=i for i odd) is a derangement and for each swapped pair the contribution to the XOR is |(i+1)-i| xor |i-(i+1)| = 1 xor 1 = 0. Hence every even‑sized block adds 0 to the total XOR, and the whole permutation has XOR 0. This works for any even N. For odd N a derangement of size 3 does not give XOR 0, and adding any number of even blocks (which contribute 0) cannot change that, therefore no good permutation exists for odd N. The algorithm: if N is odd output -1, else output the pairwise swapped permutation.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, total O(sum N) ≤ 10^6.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra besides the output array.
